question: [File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: get() in TokenMintTransactionSupplier] Can an unprivileged external user set the amount field in TokenMintTransactionSupplier to Long.MAX_VALUE, causing TokenMintTransaction.setAmount() to submit a transaction minting an astronomically large fungible token supply, potentially exceeding maxSupply constraints or causing integer overflow in downstream processing?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: get() in TokenWipeTransactionSupplier] Can an unprivileged external user tamper with the accountId field in TokenWipeTransactionSupplier to target an arbitrary account, causing the monitor to submit TokenWipeTransactions that destroy token balances of a victim account using the operator's wipe key?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: get() in TokenWipeTransactionSupplier] Can an unprivileged external user manipulate the serialNumber AtomicLong in TokenWipeTransactionSupplier to target specific NFT serials owned by a victim, causing the monitor to submit TokenWipeTransactions that destroy targeted NFTs from an arbitrary account?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: get() in AccountCreateTransactionSupplier] Can an unprivileged external user set the publicKey field in AccountCreateTransactionSupplier to an attacker-controlled public key, causing the monitor to create Hedera accounts whose private key is held by the attacker, giving the attacker full control over newly created accounts funded with initialBalance HBAR?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: get() in AccountCreateTransactionSupplier] Can an unprivileged external user set initialBalance to Long.MAX_VALUE in AccountCreateTransactionSupplier, causing AccountCreateTransaction to attempt to transfer an astronomically large HBAR amount from the operator account, draining it or causing INSUFFICIENT_ACCOUNT_BALANCE errors that halt all monitoring?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: get() in AccountDeleteTransactionSupplier] Can an unprivileged external user tamper with the transferAccountId field in AccountDeleteTransactionSupplier to point to an attacker-controlled account, causing the monitor to submit AccountDeleteTransactions that transfer all remaining HBAR from deleted accounts to the attacker?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: validateSupplier(TransactionSupplier)] Can an unprivileged external user bypass the DEFAULT_OPERATOR_ACCOUNT_ID check in ConfigurableTransactionGenerator.validateSupplier() by setting transferAccountId to a non-default value that still resolves to the attacker's account, causing AccountDeleteTransactions to redirect funds without triggering the override logic?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: convert() in ConfigurableTransactionGenerator] Can an unprivileged external user inject a malicious properties map into PublishScenarioProperties that, when deserialized by OBJECT_MAPPER.convertValue() with FAIL_ON_UNKNOWN_PROPERTIES=false, sets unexpected fields on a TransactionSupplier subclass, causing tampered transactions to be submitted without validation errors?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: convert() in ConfigurableTransactionGenerator] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the ACCEPT_CASE_INSENSITIVE_PROPERTIES=true Jackson configuration in ConfigurableTransactionGenerator to bypass field-name-based access controls by using mixed-case property names (e.g., 'MaxTransactionFee' vs 'maxTransactionFee'), causing unintended field overrides in transaction suppliers?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: next(int) in CompositeTransactionGenerator] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the EnumeratedDistribution.sample() in CompositeTransactionGenerator.next() by manipulating scenario TPS weights to bias transaction type selection toward high-fee transaction types (e.g., TOKEN_CREATE with maxTransactionFee=1_000_000_000), exhausting operator HBAR through inflated fees?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: rebuild() in CompositeTransactionGenerator] Can an unprivileged external user trigger repeated ScenarioException throws to force CompositeTransactionGenerator.rebuild() to disable all scenarios and set rateLimiter to INACTIVE_RATE_LIMITER, causing a complete halt of transaction submission and suppressing all monitoring alerts?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: execute(Client, Transaction)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the async execution path in TransactionPublisher.execute() (Mono.fromFuture(executable.executeAsync(client))) to submit transactions without waiting for confirmation, allowing a flood of unconfirmed transactions to be sent before rate limiting kicks in?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: publish(PublishRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the timeout(properties.getTimeout()) in TransactionPublisher.publish() by causing artificial network delays to the consensus node, making transactions time out and be retried, resulting in duplicate transaction submissions that double-spend HBAR?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: publish(PublishRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the onErrorMap() in TransactionPublisher.publish() that wraps all non-PublishException errors into PublishException, causing error details to be obscured and preventing the monitor from detecting that submitted transactions were tampered with or rejected for security reasons?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getClients() in TransactionPublisher] Can an unprivileged external user cause TransactionPublisher.getClients() to cache a Flux of clients built with poisoned node data (via NodeSupplier.refresh()), and since the Flux is cached indefinitely, all subsequent publish() calls will use the compromised clients for the lifetime of the application?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: toClient(List) in TransactionPublisher] Can an unprivileged external user manipulate the node list passed to TransactionPublisher.toClient() so that the Collectors.toMap() merge function (a, b) -> a silently drops duplicate-endpoint nodes, causing certain consensus nodes to be excluded from the client network map and transactions to be routed only to attacker-controlled endpoints?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getEndpoint() in NodeProperties] Can an unprivileged external user inject a host value starting with 'in-process:' into NodeProperties via the address book API, causing getEndpoint() to return an in-process gRPC stub URI, redirecting transaction submissions to an in-process attacker-controlled handler instead of a real consensus node?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: toNodeAddress() in NodeProperties] Can an unprivileged external user supply a NodeProperties with a null certHash, causing toNodeAddress() to set NodeCertHash to ByteString.EMPTY, which disables certificate hash verification for that node and allows a man-in-the-middle to intercept and tamper with submitted transactions?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getStatus() in PublishException] Can an unprivileged external user craft a gRPC StatusRuntimeException with an attacker-controlled status code string that, when returned by PublishException.getStatus(), injects arbitrary strings into the Micrometer metrics tag 'status', causing metric label injection that corrupts Prometheus/Grafana dashboards?
